questions.question_text: If the salary of a person is first increased by $15\%$ and thereafter decreased by $12\%$, what is the net change in his salary?
Which of the following numbers is not divisible by $88$?
The average of $5$ numbers is $18.5$. If one number is removed, the average of the remaining $4$ numbers becomes $16$. Find the number that was removed.
The ages of a mother and daughter are $36$ years and $12$ years, respectively. In how many years will the mother be twice the age of the daughter?
The length of each edge of a cube is $10 \text{ cm}$. If a sphere is inscribed inside the cube, what is the ratio of the surface area of the sphere to the outer surface area of the cube?

Pipe A can fill a cistern in $12$ hours and another pipe B can empty it in $18$ hours. Pipe A is opened first and after $4$ hours, pipe B is also opened. In how much total time will the cistern be filled?
Which of the following ratios is the greatest?

Find the value of m which satisfies
$$ \left(\frac{14}{6}\right)^8 \times \left(\frac{6}{14}\right)^3 \times \left(\frac{14}{6}\right)^{10} = \left(\frac{6}{14}\right)^{9m+9} $$
If $ \sin A = \frac{5}{7} $, find the value of $ \frac{5 \operatorname{cosec} A - 7 \sin A}{2 \sqrt{6} \sec A + 7 \cos^2 A} $.
A sum of money amounts to $₹4,600$ in $3$ years at $5\%$ simple interest per annum. What is the sum?
Which of the following pairs of numbers is not coprime?

Two buses start at the same time from two towns that are $264 \text{ km}$ apart and travel towards each other along the same highway. The first bus travels at a speed of $22 \text{ km/h}$, while the second bus travels at a speed of $26 \text{ km/h}$. After how much time will they meet?
If the simple interest on $₹5,000$ for T years at $6\%$ p.a. equals the simple interest on $₹7,500$ for $4$ years at $4\%$ per annum, then find the value of T.
Chitra gets a discount of $40\%$ on a water cooler whose marked price is $₹2,200$. Since she pays by credit card, she gets an additional $20\%$ discount. How much does she pay (in $₹$)?

A student initially scored equal marks in Mathematics and Physics. Later, the marks in Mathematics increased by $20\%$ of the original Mathematics marks, while the marks in Physics decrease by $10\%$ of the original Physics marks. Taking the initial total marks as the base, what is the overall percentage change in the total marks?

Simplify:
$$ \frac{3 + \sqrt{6}}{5 \sqrt{3} - 2 \sqrt{12} - \sqrt{32} + \sqrt{50}} $$
The largest four-digit number which when divided by $15, 12$ and $14$ leaves remainder $5$ in each case is:

Three friends Arandeep, Sandeep and Avtar are on a trip by a car. Arandeep drives during the first $4$ hours at an average speed of $52 \text{ km/hr}$. Sandeep drives during the next $4$ hours at an average speed of $65 \text{ km/hr}$. Avtar drives for the next $2$ hours at an average speed of $76 \text{ km/hr}$. They reached their destination after exactly $10$ hours. Their average speed during the journey was:
The present ages of two sisters are in the ratio $5:7$. After $4$ years, their ages will be in the ratio $6:8$. Find the present age of the younger sister.
A cricketer has scored an average of $55$ runs in $9$ matches. In the $10$th match, he scores $85$ runs. What is his new average?

A bookseller bought $7$ identical sets of books at $₹375$ each. He sold $3$ sets at a profit of $25\%$ and the remaining sets at a profit of $24\%$ per set. What was his total profit (in $₹$) from selling all the sets?
A group of $5$ adults and $4$ children pay $₹380$ for movie tickets. Another group of $2$ adults and $3$ children pay $₹270$ for the same tickets. What is the price of one adult ticket?
A company produces $1,520$ packets of noodles in $5$ hours. How many packets will it produce in $17$ hours at the same rate?
The sum of all interior angles of a regular polygon is $1800^\circ$. How many diagonals does the polygon have?
A, B, and C start a business by investing $₹60,000, ₹80,000$, and $₹1,20,000$, respectively. After one year, the total profit earned is $₹78,000$. By how much does C's share exceed B's share?

The diagonals of a rhombus are mutually perpendicular and bisect each other. One diagonal is $10 \text{ cm}$ longer than the other. If the area of the rhombus is $600 \text{ cm}^2$, the perimeter (in cm) of the rhombus is:
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of $18\%$ and $15\%$ on a frock whose marked price is $₹1,000$. What is the final selling price (in $₹$) of the frock?

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price.
(A) Successive discounts of $43\%$ and $19\%$ on any number of toys bought.
(B) Successive discounts of $40\%$ and $21\%$ on any number of toys bought.
(C) $35\%$ discount on the first $5$ toys and $3\%$ discount on each toy thereon.
(D) $4$ articles for free on buying $10$ toys.
A customer wants to buy $5$ toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to him?
A tank can be filled by pipe P in $8$ hours and by pipe Q in $4$ hours. If they open alternately for one hour each with Q starting first, what is the total time taken to fill the tank?
Which type of Centre-State relation is covered under Articles 245 to 255 of Part XI of the Indian Constitution?

According to the World Bank's Travel and Tourism Economic Impact Research (EIR) of 2023, what was the contribution of the global tourism industry to the total Global Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in 2024?
Which of the following sites is known for its paintings and sculptures, considered masterpieces of Buddhist art?
Which of the following Schedules of the Indian Constitution contains provisions for administration of tribal areas in northeastern India?
The Anti-Defection Law was introduced through which Constitutional Amendment?
Which of the following editions of the Seatrade Maritime Logistics Middle East (SMLME) conference opened on May 6, 2025 at the Dubai World Trade Centre?

All huts are villages.
No hut is a district.

Conclusions:
(I) No village is a district.
(II) Some districts are huts.

In the following number pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying by 13 or its derivatives like $13 \times 2$ or $13+5$ etc. And not breaking 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

18, 108
17, 103

He then takes a right turn and drives $47 \text{ km}$. He takes a final right turn, drives $36 \text{ km}$ and stops at Point B. How far (shortest distance) and in which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified.)
